pattern: DP with three remainder buckets — track the maximum achievable sum whose remainder mod 3 is 0, 1, or 2 respectively, updating all three states for every element.
hints: Think about what information you need to carry forward: at each step you only care about the best total reachable for each possible remainder (0, 1, 2), not the full history of choices.

When you process a new number, its contribution shifts the remainder of every existing sum by (existing_remainder + num) % 3 — so each of the three buckets fans out into a candidate for a potentially different bucket.
After scanning all numbers the answer sits in remainder-0 bucket; initialise the other two buckets to negative infinity (or -1) so that a partially-filled state never contaminates the final answer.

edge_inputs.description: all elements already divisible by three
single element not divisible by three — answer is zero
must skip the smallest remainder-1 element to make sum divisible
two remainder-2 elements must both be excluded when no remainder-1 candidate is cheaper
all elements have remainder 1 — take multiples-of-3 count worth
all elements have remainder 2 — take pairs
mixed with several zeros among values — zeros are always safe to include
greedy total-sum approach fails: removing one elem vs two changes answer

stress.description: large random array — exercises all three remainder buckets under maximum load
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 40000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
